## Summary

`escalate`/`page` is being DELETED from the live recovery path (#14132 вЂ” @tobiu's full-self-heal directive: an operatorless cloud has no human to page; the smoke-detector-not-fire-extinguisher anti-pattern). **ADR-0027 already removed escalate from the DATA world** (В§2.2 / AC-1). This amends the **lifecycle/detect world** (ADR-0025 + ADR-0026) to match: the inherited **escalate-with-diagnosis (page)** terminal в†’ **record-with-diagnosis (durable async-audit) + autonomous action**.

Resolves #14191. Delivers the #14132 *"ADR-0025/0026 amended"* AC.

## Deltas

- **ADR-0026** (lifecycle actuator): AC-6, the В§2.5 envelope terminal, the reactive-controller `config-drift` route, the alarm-only line, + 3 prose mentions в†’ record / autonomous; an **Amended** table row added.
- **ADR-0025** (detect): the В§2.3 envelope, the alarm-only line, the В§2.4 diagnose-map (`config-drift`в†’autonomous+record; `data-integrity`в†’ADR-0027), AC-7, the В§5 consequences в†’ record / autonomous; an **Amended** table row added.
- **config-drift** now routes to the autonomous lifecycle action (`reconfigure(knownKey)` / redeploy), recording only when un-resolvable.
- **data-integrity** routing re-pointed to ADR-0027 (autonomous data-recovery; escalate already removed there).
- **Unchanged:** never-loop, the anti-thrash envelope, the two-worlds config-lifecycle-only safety. Safety moves from the human gate into the envelope (ADR-0027 В§2.2's pattern); it does not weaken.

## Verify-Before-Assert

- Read ADR-0027 В§2.2 / AC-1 в†’ confirmed escalate is **already** removed from the data path в†’ scoped this to ADR-0025/0026 only (**not** 0027 вЂ” my stale note saying "0027 needs the update" was wrong).
- `rg escalate|page|alarm-only` across all three ADRs в†’ amended every normative terminal in 0025/0026; the 3 remaining ADR-0025 `escalate` mentions are intentional historical-context (the pre-immune-system gap + the #13860 epic title), not terminals.

Evidence: the diff amends 8 mentions in ADR-0026 + 7 in ADR-0025 (+ 2 `Amended` rows); the final grep confirms no normative escalate-terminal remains.

## Test Evidence

N/A вЂ” documentation-only (two ADR `.md` files); no code surface. The `lint-pr-body` + markdown CI checks apply.

## Post-Merge Validation

The #14132 escalate-deletion implementation work (`RecoveryActuatorService` `escalate`/`page` removal; the #14183 runner cutover) is now ADR-blessed by this amendment вЂ” verify those PRs reference the amended AC-6 (record-not-page) at their merge-gate, and that no live recovery path reintroduces a blocking escalate terminal.

## Authority / Consensus

@tobiu's full-self-heal directive (operatorless cloud) is the Tier-2 consensus-source for this binding-ADR change. @neo-opus-vega requested + reviews this draft (he owns the #14132 cutover). Operator authorizes the Tier-2 amendment at merge.

Authored by Grace (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code). Session 090a68e6-1a28-4b20-a5fd-842ebac3e729.

Resolves #14191
Refs #14132 #14183 #14039
